sections.paragraph:
Γεννησαρέτ indecl., ἡ (more correctly Γεννησάρ as 1 Macc 11:67; Joseph., Talmud; so also the foll. witnesses in Mt and Mk: D, It., Syr. Sin. and Cur., Pesh.; s. RHarris, ET 40, 1929, 189f) Gennesaret, prob. name of the fertile and (in I a.d.) thickly populated plain south of Capernaum, now El-Ghuweir (Jos., Bell. 3, 516ff) Mt 14:34; Mk 6:53. This was also the name of the large lake adjacent to the plain, λίμνη Γ. (Jos., Bell. 3, 506 λίμνη Γεννησάρ; 1 Macc 11:67 τὸ ὕδωρ τοῦ Γεννησάρ; Stephan. Byz. s.v. Τιβεριάς: this is a city πρὸς τῇ Γεννεσιρίτιδι λίμνῃ) Lk 5:1, less precisely termed θάλασσα τῆς Γαλιλαίας (Mt 4:18; Mk 1:16), and θάλ. τῆς Τιβεριάδος (J 21:1).—Dalman, Orte3 118 (Eng. tr. 121–22); Westm. Hist. Atlas 17 etc.; CKopp, Holy Places of the Gospels, ’63, 167–203; BHHW I 546f.

indecl. indecl. = indeclinable

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

foll. foll. = followed, following

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

It. It. = Itala, early version of the NT in Latin

Syr. Syr. = Syriac

ET ET = Expository Times—List 6

prob. prob. = probable, probably

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Byz. Byz. = Byzantine, esp. in ref. to papyri

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Dalman Dalman = GD., various works—List 6

Eng. tr. Eng. tr. = English translation

Hist.

Hist. (after a personal name)=historicus, historian

hist. = historian, historical

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 194
